name: Brynjar Andyrsson
whyHere: You put down the blade, or you were sent away from it, or you are looking for a line worth holding that isn't theirs.

inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

inventory.name: Waterskin
inventory.description: Boiled and refilled at the last clean spring. Worth more every week the river runs gray.

inventory.name: Cloth Bundle
inventory.description: Folded pale linen containing a dried white-flowered sprig and a length of cord knotted in an unrecognized pattern. Left at the Crow and Thistle's third stool the night Sera was present. Freki read it as a live trail heading east.

deeds.description: Swore a blood oath on his father Andyr's name to seek honorable vengeance against Jarl Kjotve Olafsson
Approached a Redline Warrior as a peer, named the task plainly, and earned his name and his company

turns.summary: Brynjar arrives in the Marchward — a border town between war and false peace — and takes his first look at the square.
turns.narration:
The Marchward sits at the edge of two worlds and looks like neither. It is a working town — stone buildings with patched roofs, a main street packed to mud by boot and cart, cookfire smoke threading straight up in the still spring air. The snow is gone but the cold lingers in the shade, and the light has that early-season flatness where everything looks a little undecided.

You came through the gate road from the north two hours ago, and the town noticed you the way a border town notices everyone: a look, a pause, a resumed conversation. A scarred Redline warrior with a jawbone necklace ate his breakfast on a bench and watched you pass without expression. A woman in grey Stillhold cloth crossed without making eye contact. Freki walked at your heel and watched both, ears forward.

You are standing now at the edge of the Marchward's central square — more of a wide junction than a proper square, but there is a well here, and a public board with bills tacked to it, and a low building to your left marked WARDHOUSE in worn paint. Across the square a tavern, the hearthfire visible through an open shutter. To your right, the road continues east toward the Redline; behind you, the road runs west toward the Stillhold. A waygate anchor-stone stands at the square's far corner, old and plain and exactly where the treaty says it must be — nobody stands near it.

Freki puts her nose to the mud and circles the well twice, then sits beside you and looks up at your face.

turns.player: I’ll find a tree and rest against
turns.summary: Brynjar settles against a birch at the square's edge to take the measure of the Marchward.
turns.narration:
You find a birch at the square's edge, bark white and cold, and put your back to it. Freki circles once and settles against your left leg, the solid warmth of him a thing you've stopped noticing the way you stop noticing your own heartbeat. From here you can see the whole square without looking like you're watching it.

The Redline warrior finishes his breakfast and sets the bowl on the bench beside him. He doesn't move. His eyes track the square the same way yours do — not suspicious, just professional. You've met that look before, in men who've spent enough time in dangerous places that vigilance became habit and habit became muscle. His jawbone necklace is old, polished smooth from handling. Someone's, once.

The notice board has three bills tacked to it. At this distance you can make out the shapes but not the words. One is a long column of text, black ink, official-looking. Another is hand-lettered and recent — the ink still dark. The Wardhouse door behind the warrior is closed but not barred; there's a strip of lamplight showing under it even now, which means someone's already at work inside.

Freki's ear — the notched one — rotates toward the tavern. Something in there: voices, the scratch of a bench on stone, the particular note of people already in an argument before the morning's properly started.

turns.player: I’ll sit against the tree with Freki and think back about everything I’ve experienced, the raid on Javiksholm, the death of my father by Kjotves hand. Think about the burning hatred and lust for revenge.
turns.summary: Brynjar sits against a birch in the Marchward square and turns the memory of the raid over in his hands — his father's death, Kjotve's cowardice, the weight of unfinished business.
turns.narration:
The birch bark is cold through your shirt. Freki's weight is warm against your leg, steady as breathing.

You close your eyes and it comes easy — too easy. The raid doesn't take effort to recall. It comes back the way cold water comes back when you've been under it: all at once, total. The sound first — not the clash of weapons but the dogs barking, then stopping. The smell of thatch catching. Then the light of it, orange and wrong through the smoke. Your father's voice saying *go*, the single flat word that meant he'd already decided how this ended.

You looked back. You shouldn't have, but you did. You watched Kjotve Olafsson move — not like a warrior caught in a fight, but like a man ticking a task off a list. Andyr was still standing when the spear went into his back from behind. Not Kjotve's hand. Kjotve's *order*. That detail sits in your chest like a bone splinter, the specific cowardice of it. The duel was real until Kjotve was losing, and then it stopped being a duel, and Andyr fell, and Kjotve stood over him and said something you couldn't hear across the burning square.

Freki shifts, pushing his flank harder against your leg. You open your eyes. The square is still here — the warrior on the bench, the anchor-stone at the far corner, cookfire smoke going straight up in cold still air.

The hatred isn't hot. That's what surprises you, still, when you look at it directly. It doesn't burn. It sits heavy and patient in your ribcage the way a stone sits at the bottom of still water. Kjotve has the village, the fields, the shore. He has Andyr's land and Andyr's name on no grave. And you are here, on the far side of the world, alive because your father told you to be.

You don't know yet what you're going to do with that. But you know it isn't finished.

turns.player: I’ll swear to myself and in somewhat my father, “I will kill Kjotve for what he has done. I will kill him but I will do it with more honor than that spineless serpent has ever had. I swear it, to all the gods above and especially to you father.” I’ll scratch Freki behind the ears once more and pat him in the head. Then, I’ll stand and walk over to the square.
turns.summary: Brynjar swears a blood oath on his father's name — to kill Kjotve with more honor than the Jarl ever showed — then walks into the Marchward square.
turns.narration:
You speak it quiet enough that only Freki hears — and the birch bark behind you, and whatever gods are awake at this hour in a border town between war and false peace. *I will kill Kjotve for what he has done. I will kill him but I will do it with more honor than that spineless serpent has ever had. I swear it, to all the gods above and especially to you, father.* The words feel different said aloud. Heavier. A thing you can't take back, which is exactly what you wanted.

Freki tilts his head into your hand when you scratch behind his ears — that slow lean into pressure, the small surrender of it. You pat the broad skull once and he holds still under it, watching the square with you for one more moment, like he understood the weight of what just passed.

You stand. The cold comes back into your legs from the packed earth. You roll your shoulders once and walk out into the square proper — mud underfoot, the smell of cookfire and early morning damp, the well at the center and the notice board beside it. Three bills tacked to the board, edges already curling in the spring air. The Redline warrior on the bench near the Wardhouse turns his head a fraction when you cross toward the well — not a challenge, just the same professional accounting he's been running all morning. From here you can hear the tavern argument more clearly: a woman's voice, sharp and certain, and a lower reply that keeps trying to end the conversation and failing.

turns.player: Read the notice board — see what the Marchward is posting this morning
turns.summary: Brynjar reads the Marchward notice board — a labor posting, a truce-law warning, a missing Stillholder woman — and a Redline warrior offers him an unsolicited and accurate assessment of his fighting form.
turns.narration:
The notice board is three steps from the well, low enough that someone short could read it without standing on their toes. Three bills, edges curling from overnight damp.

The first is a work posting — the Wardhouse wants a second hand for road-clearing on the eastern track toward the Stillhold, two days' labor, meal and copper included. Someone has already written TAKEN across it in charcoal and underlined it twice.

The second is a warning, block-lettered in a confident hand: WAYGATE TRANSIT — ALL ARRIVING PARTIES MUST PRESENT THEMSELVES TO THE WARDHOUSE WITHIN THE HOUR. ARMS CHECKED AT THE ANCHOR. THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL. Below it, smaller: Violation is grounds for removal. The Marchward holds its truce by agreement, not courtesy.

The third is older than the other two — the tack holding its upper-left corner has gone green with rust. It reads: MISSING. Sera of the Stillhold, grey cloak, dark braid, last seen at the south gate seven days past. Her family asks only that word be sent. No payment named. No questions asked of the finder. Below the text, a careful ink drawing of a woman's face — composed, watchful eyes, a detail of the cloak's hem-pattern for identification.

Freki noses the base of the board, smelling whoever stood here last. He doesn't growl.

Then the Redline warrior is beside you. Not close — a respectful arm's length — but you didn't hear him cross the square, which is the kind of thing worth noting. He reads the board for three seconds, not the bills, just looking in their direction while he says, without turning his head: "You lead from your left shoulder. Gap above the collarbone when you raise your arm. Someone patient would put a blade there before the second exchange." A pause. "You have time to close it." He goes back to his bench.

turns.player: Go to the Wardhouse and present yourself as required
turns.summary: Brynjar presents himself at the Wardhouse as truce-law requires and is logged by the duty officer.
turns.narration:
The Wardhouse is a low stone building on the north side of the square, lamplight still burning under the door even though the morning is pale enough to read by. You push it open.

Inside: a long table, a rack of iron hooks holding two shortswords and a crossbow that someone checked before you, a charcoal map of the Marchward pinned flat with river stones. A woman sits at the far end of the table, stylus in hand, a ledger open in front of her. She looks up when you and Freki come in. Her eyes go to the axe on your hip, then to the shield across your back, then to your face. Unhurried, thorough.

"Name and origin," she says. No pleasantry, no threat in it — just the form. She dips the stylus and waits. Behind you, Freki sniffs at the threshold and then steps fully inside, tail low and steady.

You give her Brynjar Andyrsson, Javiksholm. She writes it. When she looks up again there's something in her expression — not pity, not recognition, just the slight weight of someone who has heard the name of a razed village before and knows what a man from one looks like when he walks in. She doesn't comment on it. She gestures at the hooks on the wall. "Arms stay here while you're in the square on Marchward business. You can pick them up when you leave or check them for the night. Your call." She marks something in the ledger. "Anything to declare — cargo, message, blood business?"

turns.player: Check your weapons at the hooks and ask if there's paid work in the Marchward
turns.summary: Brynjar checks his weapons at the Wardhouse hooks and learns of two opportunities: a night-watch posting and the missing-person bill for Sera of the Stillhold.
turns.narration:
The axe comes off your hip with a practiced shrug — you set it handle-up on the nearest hook, then work the shield from your back and hang it flat against the wall. The rack takes both without ceremony. The woman behind the ledger watches your hands the whole time, and when the shield settles she marks something brief in the margin.

"Brynjar Andyrsson," she says, as if confirming it against the entry she already made. "From Javiksholm." A pause — not unkind, just the gap where the next question usually sits. "Blood business, cargo, or message — anything to declare?"

Freki has stationed himself near the door, nose working the air of the place: old iron, lamp oil, someone's cold breakfast. His ears are low but not flat. Neutral ground reads as neutral to him.

You answer her question, and then put yours to her plainly: is there paid work in the Marchward? She doesn't look up from the ledger at first — finishes the line she's writing — then sets the stylus down and gives you the honest answer this place is known for.

"Road-clearing east toward the Stillhold is taken. What's open—" she turns a page, scans it, turns it back "—is a night-watch slot. The Wardhouse posts one vacancy a week for travelers with no permanent standing here. Meal, floor space, and a half-copper per watch. It's not glory." She looks at you steadily. "And there's the missing-person bill on the board. No official work in that — but the Marchward would notice if someone brought Sera of the Stillhold back through the south gate."

turns.player: Ask Halvra what she knows about the missing woman — the bill said no questions, but she'd know more than the board
turns.summary: Halvra fills in what the bill left out — Sera arrived alone, checked in properly, and vanished overnight; the Crow and Thistle is the thread to pull.
turns.narration:
Halvra looks at you for a moment before she answers — weighing, you think, not whether to tell you, but how much is useful.

"Sera," she says. "Stillhold family, respectable. Came through the gate seven days back, alone, which is unusual — the Stillhold doesn't send its people out alone unless they have a reason they won't name." She sets the stylus down and folds her hands on the ledger. "She checked in properly, declared no blood business, no cargo. Said she was looking for lodging. I logged her, pointed her toward the Crow and Thistle." A pause. "Next morning, her room was empty and the innkeep hadn't seen her leave. The bill went up that afternoon."

Freki has drifted closer to the table during this — not begging, just listening. Halvra glances at him once and says nothing about it.

"The bill says no questions of the finder," she adds, "which the family wrote themselves. My read is they know roughly what she came here to do, and they'd rather have her back than an accounting." She picks up the stylus again, not dismissively — just ready to write whatever comes next. "The Crow and Thistle would be the starting place. Innkeep's name is Doss. He's thorough when he wants to be."

She leaves it there, open.

turns.player: I'll ask Halvra "Are there any caravans or important individuals needing protection travelling through the town? Any bodyguard jobs listed?"
turns.summary: Halvra tells Brynjar that bodyguard postings aren't formal here — the Crow and Thistle is where those conversations happen.
turns.narration:
Halvra picks the stylus back up and taps it once against the ledger's edge — thinking, or just giving the question its due weight.

"Bodyguard work." She says it flat, not dismissive. "Nothing posted formally, no. The Marchward doesn't run a hire-board for that — too easy to abuse on neutral ground." She glances toward the square-side window, a narrow rectangle of pale morning light. "What comes through here, mostly, are travelers who already have their arrangements made before they arrive. Redline folk with escorts, Stillhold messengers in pairs." A beat. "Occasionally someone comes through who didn't plan as well as they thought and starts asking the same question you just asked me."

She turns a page in the ledger — not dismissively, just continuing her work while she talks. "The Crow and Thistle is where you'd hear about it before I would. Doss keeps an ear on everything that moves through his common room. If a merchant came in last night nervous about the road east, Doss would know before they'd finished their first cup." She looks up again, direct. "You're already going there for the Sera business, unless I misread you."

turns.player: Head to the Crow and Thistle — ask Doss about Sera's disappearance and whether anyone needs a road escort east
turns.summary: Brynjar retrieves his weapons and crosses to the Crow and Thistle; Doss the innkeep sizes him up immediately and names both his reasons for being there before he can.
turns.narration:
You lift your axe and shield from the hooks — Halvra logs the checkout with a single mark, no comment — and step back into the square. The morning is cool and damp, mud soft underfoot from the melt, the well rope beaded with condensation. Freki falls in at your left heel the moment you clear the Wardhouse door, tail low, reading the square before you do.

The Crow and Thistle sits on the south side, a two-story building with a patched roof and a painted sign that has weathered into something closer to a smear than a bird. The door stands open to clear the night's smoke. Inside: low ceiling, four long tables, a fire banked down to orange coals, and the smell of barley mash and tallow. Two men eat at the far end without looking up. Behind the bar a stout woman in her fifties is restacking cups — she watches Freki clear the threshold before she watches you, which tells you something about her.

"Doss?" you ask.

She sets a cup down. "The same." Her eyes move over the axe on your hip, the shield at your back, the hound beside you, and settle on your face. "Bit early for a drink. Bit early for whatever else you're selling too, probably." Not unfriendly — the Marchward's particular brand of honesty, plain enough that it doesn't need to be rude. "You're here about the Stillhold girl, or you're here about the east road. Or both, which would make you either useful or trouble."

Freki walks slowly along the bar's length, nose working the floor. He stops at a spot near the far stool — the third seat from the left — and looks up at you once, then looks back at the floor. Doss watches him do it. She doesn't say anything about it, but her expression changes slightly, the way a careful person's does when they've just noticed something worth thinking about.

turns.player: Ask about Sera directly — what did Doss see, what did she hear, and does she think the girl left on her own?
turns.summary: Doss confirms Sera left of her own will before dawn; Freki stops at the third stool from the left and Doss notices.
turns.narration:
Doss sets down the cup she was wiping and looks at you with the particular patience of someone who has answered this question before, or knew it was coming.

"She left on her own," Doss says. "That much I'm certain of. The door to her room was latched from the inside — you can only do that from inside. Window was shut. I run a clean house." She says that last part without emphasis, which makes it land harder. "She came down before I was up, which is early, and she went out the side door. I heard it. Didn't think anything of it — some folk walk at dawn." She picks the cup back up and resumes. "Didn't come back for breakfast. Didn't come back at all."

"She was quiet," Doss adds, like she's been deciding whether it matters. "Not frightened-quiet. Thinking-quiet. Sat right there—" a tip of her chin toward the middle of the room, "—ate her supper alone, had one cup, watched the door the whole time. Waited for someone, maybe, or was watching to see if someone walked in she didn't want to see. Couldn't tell you which."

Freki has moved along the bar slowly, unhurried, the way it moves when it's actually interested in something rather than just alert. It stops at the third stool from the left and stands there — not sniffing, just standing, head down, weight forward. Doss watches the dog with more attention than she's given you since you walked in.

"Your hound does that every time something's been left behind," she says. Not a question.

turns.player: Ask what was left behind at that stool — someone sat there around the time Sera did
turns.summary: Doss reveals what was left at the third stool — a sprig of white herb and a knotted cord — and offers it to Brynjar; Freki reads the spot with focused attention.
turns.narration:
You nod toward the third stool. "Something was left there," you say. "Around the time Sera was here. What was it?"

Doss doesn't look surprised you asked. She reaches under the bar and sets a small object on the worn wood between you — a folded square of cloth, pale linen, the kind someone uses to wrap a keepsake. She unfolds it with two fingers. Inside is a single dried sprig of something — white-flowered, small, the kind of herb you'd find at the Stillhold's edge if you knew where to look — and a piece of cord knotted in a pattern you don't immediately recognize. No note. No name.

"Someone sat there the same evening she did," Doss says. "Not together — separate stools, never spoke that I saw. But they were watching each other. The person left before she did, left this behind." She refolds the linen. "Could be a coincidence. Could be a message she knew how to read and I don't."

Freki has not moved from the stool. The hound stands with its weight forward, head lowered slightly, reading something in the wood or the air above it — not distress, not alarm, but the focused attention it gives a trail worth following. You've learned to trust that posture.

"You want it?" Doss asks, pushing the folded cloth toward you. "I've no use for it. And I'd rather it go with someone who's actually looking for her."

turns.player: Let Freki lead — take the cloth, hold it for the hound, and follow wherever it wants to go
turns.summary: Freki reads the cloth bundle and tracks toward the side door — east, toward a fork between the waygate approach and the Stillhold road.
turns.narration:
You pick up the cloth bundle — the linen still faintly cool, the dried sprig stiff between your fingers — and crouch down to Freki's level. The hound has not moved from the stool. You hold the bundle out low, close to its nose, and wait.

Freki takes one long breath. Then another. Its head drops, nostrils working the cord-knot first, then the sprig, then the cloth itself. After a moment it lifts its head and turns — not toward the door you came in, but toward the side door. The one Doss said Sera used before dawn.

Doss watches this without comment. When Freki moves toward the side door at a slow, deliberate walk — head low, tail straight, the working posture you have come to recognize as distinct from curiosity — she says, quietly, "That door opens onto the lane behind the building. Goes east, then splits. North fork is the waygate approach. South fork joins the Stillhold road."

Freki stops at the door and looks back at you. The bundle is still in your hand. The two men at the far table have not looked up from their breakfast, and Doss has not moved from behind the bar — but she is watching you with the focused stillness of someone deciding something about you.

turns.player: I'll follow Freki and his intuition, but first I'll stop and talk to the Redline Warrior, ask for his name and ask if he'd care to join me in searching the Sera.
turns.summary: Brynjar recruits Ragnar Solmyr at the bench — the Redline Warrior names himself and confirms he saw movement at the east fork before dawn; both head for the side door.
turns.narration:
Freki holds at the side door, patient, head still low. You hold up a hand toward the hound — wait — and cross back through the common room, past the two men eating without much interest in you, and out the main entrance into the morning square.

The Redline Warrior is where he was — bench near the Wardhouse, forearms on his knees, watching the square the way a man watches a river for fish. He clocks you before you're halfway across. His eyes go to your shoulder first, the same gap he named yesterday, then to your face.

You stop a step away — close enough to be direct, not so close it reads as a challenge. "Looking for a woman who left before dawn and didn't say where," you say. "Trail goes east from the inn's side door — could fork north toward the waygate or south toward the Stillhold road. Two directions to cover and a dog with a nose, but I don't know this ground yet." You meet his eyes. "I'd know your name before I asked you to walk with me."

He looks at you for a long beat. Then he straightens off the bench — not fast, not slow, just the economy of someone whose body doesn't move without reason — and says: "Ragnar Solmyr." The jawbone necklace shifts with the movement. "I've been watching that fork since yesterday. Someone went through before the town was up." He says it plainly, as if handing you a fact he'd been waiting to give someone who earned it. "I'll walk east with you. If it goes south, I know the Stillhold road four miles out."

*✦ The Marchers: +1 — approached a Redline Warrior with plain speech and a named purpose — handled the form correctly.*

turns.player: Get moving immediately — Freki's trail is cooling; trust Ragnar to keep up
turns.summary: Brynjar, Ragnar, and Freki leave the Marchward by the east lane and follow the trail to the fork, where Freki turns south toward the Stillhold road.
turns.narration:
Ragnar falls into step without a word as you cross back through the square. You push the side door open — Freki is exactly where you left it, head low, the cord-knot's scent still fresh in its nose — and the hound moves the moment your boot clears the threshold.

The east lane is narrow, mud-soft from the melt, and quiet. No one else is out this side of the Marchward yet. Freki sets a steady pace, not a run — purposeful, nose working low to the ground between strides, occasionally checking a wheel-rut or a boot-print pressed into the mud. You can see the fork maybe a hundred yards ahead: the lane splits, one branch curving north toward the tree-line where the waygate approach begins, the other bending south and wide toward the Stillhold road.

Ragnar moves beside you at the same unhurried pace, scanning the lane's edges without being asked. "Movement I saw was south-fork," he says quietly. "Two people, maybe. Before the light was full." He doesn't embellish it, doesn't say which two, doesn't say where they went after the fork — just gives you what he has, plainly, the way someone hands over a tool.

Freki slows as the fork approaches, nose dropping almost to the ground. At the split it pauses, circles once, then angles south — toward the Stillhold road.

turns.player: Pause at the fork — study the ground before committing south (boot-prints, weight, how many, how long ago)
turns.summary: At the fork, Brynjar reads two sets of prints heading south — a lighter frame first, a heavier follower ten minutes behind — and glimpses something faintly luminous pressed into the mud.
turns.narration:
Freki stops at the fork and looks back at you — not impatient, just waiting. Ragnar posts himself at the edge of the north branch, watching that lane's shadows while you crouch over the south-branch ground.

The mud here is churned enough to read. Two sets of boots came through, both heading south — you can trace the stride-length and the depth of heel-strike to get a rough picture. One set presses harder on the outside edge, a lighter frame moving fast. The other is heavier, wider-toed, a deliberate pace. They were not walking together: the lighter prints come first, the heavier ones overlay them in two or three places, maybe ten minutes behind. How long ago is harder. The melt has been slow this morning and the prints' edges are still crisp enough that you put it at three hours past, maybe four — well before the Marchward was up. But the northbound lane is wet too, and you can't rule out a third person who kept to the firmer ground near the verge.

Ragnar doesn't move from his post. "What does it say?" he asks, voice low, eyes still on the lane ahead.

"Two people," you say. "One ahead of the other. Light one went first." You stand, brushing mud from your knee. South, toward the Stillhold road, four miles to where Ragnar knows it well — and the trail still live.

About twenty yards down the south branch, a thread of something glints in the mud, half-pressed under a boot-print. Not metal. More like the brief, sourceless shimmer you sometimes see under still water on a clear morning — a pale line of light lying quietly in the earth, gone before you can look directly at it, the kind of thing that was never meant for anyone's eyes and doesn't know you're there.

turns.player: Hold up the cloth bundle again — let Freki confirm the lighter set is Sera's and set pace south
turns.summary: Freki confirms the lighter prints as Sera's and leads south toward the Stillhold road; Ragnar reads the gait and falls in.
turns.narration:
You crouch and hold the cloth bundle low, linen unfolded so the white-flowered sprig catches the air. Freki steps in without being asked. It drops its nose to the bundle — a long, deliberate draw, nostrils working — then swings its head south and presses its muzzle to the lighter set of prints. One sniff, two. It lifts its head and looks at you with the flat certainty of an animal that does not hedge.

That's her.

Ragnar takes one last scan up the north branch, then moves to your shoulder. "Light-footed one," he says, reading the ground for himself, his voice low and matter-of-fact. "She was moving, not walking. Knows where she's going or afraid of being seen — same gait either way." He doesn't say anything else. He doesn't need to.

Freki is already three yards south, picking a line between the deeper ruts where the mud holds a print cleanest. It moves at a tracking pace — steady, head low, covering ground without rushing — and you recognize the shape of it: the hound has the scent and it won't lose it unless the trail goes cold or wet. The Stillhold road is four miles out. At this pace, an hour and a half, maybe less. The figure ahead — Sera, or whoever carries her scent — had a three-to-four-hour lead and a lighter step. The heavier follower is the variable.

turns.player: Use Read the Fight on the prints and the surrounding verge — look for tells of intent in the way the heavier figure was moving (a hunter's crouch, a soldier's set, something rushing)
turns.summary: Brynjar reads the heavier follower's prints and confirms a deliberate pursuit — someone who knew Sera was ahead and was closing the gap.
turns.narration:
You set the cloth bundle aside and let your eyes go wide — not at the prints themselves but at the whole picture they make. Read the Fight is not for tracking; it is for the moment before the first blow, the way a body telegraphs what it intends before it commits. You borrow that same read and apply it to the mud.

The heavier set of prints tells you something useful. The stride is even, not erratic — no rushing, no stumble into a run. But the toe-pressure is front-loaded: this person was leaning forward, weight carried ahead of the heel, the way someone moves when they are closing distance deliberately. Not a soldier's march, which keeps the weight centred. Not a hunter's crouch, which would show scuffed dirt where they paused to still themselves. This is purposeful approach — a body that knows its target is ahead and is eating the gap between them. The spacing between prints narrows slightly as the trail bends south, which means they picked up pace as the lane straightened and sight-lines opened. Following, not coinciding.

Ragnar is watching you work. When you stand he reads your face and gives one slow nod. "Someone who knew she was ahead of them," he says. It is not a question.

Freki is already moving — three yards south, waiting, tail low and straight.

*✦ The Thread of A Northern Village Called Javiksholm But It Was Raided And Razed. — a step forward.*

turns.player: Press south at pace — Freki leads, cover ground before the trail cools further
turns.summary: The party presses south at pace; Freki holds the trail; the shimmer in the heavier prints is examined in passing — sourceless, logged, unexplained.
turns.narration:
You signal Freki with a low click of the tongue and it moves — no hesitation, nose working the south lane, tail held straight behind it. The road here is churned spring mud over packed chalk, and it holds every print clearly: Sera's light stride pressing the outside edge, the heavier follower's toe-heavy weight cutting in behind her. The two tracks run parallel for thirty yards before the mud softens and the gap between them narrows.

Ragnar falls in on your right without a word, putting his shoulder to the tree-line where the road narrows. He moves quiet for a big man — the kind of quiet that is not effort but habit. The verge here is elder scrub and last year's bramble, the first green buds just breaking. Nothing has moved through it today; the only recent passage is the road itself.

The shimmer you glimpsed from twenty yards back is now underfoot. It is not the mud catching light — the sky is flat and overcast, nothing to catch. It sits pressed into a heel-strike in the heavier set of prints, faint and sourceless, the color of water seen through smoked glass. Freki pauses over it for a breath, its nostrils widening, then moves on. Not a threat read — something it logged and set aside.

South of you the road opens into a low, damp straightaway before bending east. No figures visible. The prints continue. Freki is moving at a ground-eating trot now, confident — Sera's trail still live enough to hold.

lastTurn.sceneSummary: The party presses south at pace; Freki holds the trail; the shimmer in the heavier prints is examined in passing — sourceless, logged, unexplained.

choices.label: Crouch over the shimmer now — examine it properly before it fades further
Keep moving — ask Ragnar what he makes of it as you walk, without breaking pace
Trust Freki's read — it flagged the shimmer and moved on; push south hard and close the distance on Sera
Pull Freki back to the shimmer on lead — let the hound work it properly before you commit further south

scene.setting: The south branch of the east fork, now forty yards past the split. Early spring morning, overcast and flat-lit. The Stillhold road lies roughly four miles ahead. The shimmer is now underfoot — pressed into a heel-strike in the heavier set of prints.

npcsPresent.state: Moving on the right side of the road, watching the tree-line, quiet and deliberate.

npcsPresent.name: Freki
npcsPresent.state: Thirty yards ahead and accelerating, nose down on Sera's lighter trail.

quests.title: The Thread of A Northern Village Called Javiksholm But It Was Raided And Razed.
quests.premise: Brynjar Andyrsson carries an unfinished thing: They were forced to flee from the raid that killed their father and razed their home village.. The valley will give them the chance to face it.
quests.stakes: who they become when the thing they left behind finally catches up

stages.objective: a first sign of They were forced to flee from the raid that killed their father and razed their home village. surfaces in the valley
the thread deepens — what it costs to pull it becomes clear
the reckoning — and the choice of what to do with it

routes.marcher: answer it as your people would
routes.seek: seek the truth of it, wherever it leads

progress: Freki's trail leads south toward the Stillhold road — Sera was not alone at the fork, and the hunt is moving
Brynjar reads the heavier follower's prints as deliberate pursuit — a body leaning into the chase, closing distance with intent, pace quickening as sight-lines opened.
↳ Brynjar used Read the Fight on the pursuit trail and established a concrete lead — a deliberate follower closing on Sera, south toward the Stillhold road.

kjotve-olafsson.name: Kjotve Olafsson
history: [d1] Known from what has passed between you.

wardhouse-officer.name: Halvra Stenn
wardhouse-officer.description: Compact, unhurried, reads a man the way she reads a ledger — top to bottom, nothing missed. No warmth she hasn't decided to spend.
history: [d1] Logged Brynjar Andyrsson from Javiksholm; noted the razed-village weight without naming it; asked for a declaration of cargo, message, or blood business
[d1] Informed Brynjar of the night-watch vacancy and mentioned the missing-person bill for Sera of the Stillhold — laid out both opportunities plainly
[d1] Told Brynjar what she knows of Sera: alone, proper check-in, vanished overnight; pointed him toward Doss at the Crow and Thistle
[d1] Told Brynjar there's no formal bodyguard hire-board in the Marchward — neutral ground makes it too easy to abuse. Directed him to Doss at the Crow and Thistle for road-work arrangements.
knownFacts: Heard of Javiksholm — recognized what a man from a razed village looks like
Road-clearing east is taken; a weekly night-watch vacancy is open to travelers
The Marchward would take notice of whoever brought Sera of the Stillhold home
Sera arrived alone — unusual for a Stillholder
Sera declared no blood business or cargo, asked for lodging, was sent to the Crow and Thistle
Sera's room was empty the next morning without the innkeep seeing her leave
No formal bodyguard posting board at the Marchward
Doss at the Crow and Thistle hears about road-escort needs before the Wardhouse does
wardhouse-officer.statusNote: On duty at the Wardhouse; has shared what she knows freely and directed Brynjar toward the Crow and Thistle
wardhouse-officer.appearance: A compact woman somewhere in her mid-forties, hair the colour of old iron worn in a single braid wound close to the head. A small, neat scar through the left eyebrow — not a fighter's scar, an old workplace scar, which tells its own story. Wears the Wardhouse grey, kept better than the building deserves. Her hands are ink-stained at the fingertips and absolutely still when she is waiting for your answer.

doss.name: Doss Halveck
doss.description: Stout, fifties, unhurried behind her bar. Reads a stranger the way she reads a tab — fast and without flattery. Noticed Freki before she noticed you.
history: [d1] Greeted Brynjar before he could state his business; named both his purposes — Sera and the east road — on sight.
[d1] Confirmed Sera left on her own before dawn via the side door; described her as thinking-quiet, watching the entrance; noted Freki's behavior at the third stool
[d1] Produced the cloth bundle left at the third stool — dried white herb and a knotted cord. Offered it to Brynjar directly.
[d1] Told Brynjar the side door opens onto the east lane — north fork to the waygate, south fork to the Stillhold road. Watched him and Freki with the stillness of someone making a decision.
knownFacts: Brynjar arrived asking about Sera and road-escort work
Freki paused at the third stool from the left at the bar
Sera's room was latched from the inside — she left of her own will
Sera left before dawn via the side door
Sera ate alone, had one cup, watched the door all evening — waiting or watching for someone
A second person sat near Sera the same evening, watching her without speaking, then left before her — leaving the bundle behind
Doss kept the bundle because it felt deliberate, not lost
The side door leads east, then forks — waygate approach north, Stillhold road south
skillsObserved: quick read of a stranger's intent
doss.statusNote: On duty at the Crow and Thistle; watching Brynjar with focused consideration after Freki read the bundle.
doss.appearance: A stout woman in her mid-fifties, broad through the shoulders, heavy-handed in the way of someone who has carried kegs since she was fifteen. Skin the colour of old oak, short grey hair kept close to the skull for practicality. A crescent scar at the left jaw from a long-ago disagreement. Apron is clean — she changes it twice a day on principle. Eyes the flat, still grey of a whetstone, and about as yielding.

ragnar-solmyr.name: Ragnar Solmyr
ragnar-solmyr.role: Redline Warrior, visiting the Marchward neutral ground
ragnar-solmyr.description: Hard-built, jawbone necklace worn smooth with age. Moves without waste and watches a square the way a fisherman watches a river. Reads fighters on sight — yesterday it was your shoulder; today it is your face.
history: [d1] Named himself when asked directly and committed to walk east without haggling — offered the information that he'd seen movement at the east fork before dawn as if he'd been holding it for someone who'd earn it.
[d1] Crossed the square to deliver an accurate assessment of Brynjar's left-shoulder lead — a gap above the collarbone — then returned to his bench without waiting for a response.
[d1] Fell into step without hesitation; confirmed the movement he saw was at the south fork, two people, before full light — gave the information plainly and without embellishment
[d1] Read the lighter footprints on sight — identified fast movement versus deliberate, noted same gait for fleeing and purposeful travel. Walking at Brynjar's shoulder.
[d1] Confirmed Brynjar's read of the heavier prints — named it plainly as someone who knew Sera was ahead of them.
knownFacts: His name is Ragnar Solmyr
He observed movement at the east fork before the town was up this morning
He knows the Stillhold road four miles out
Reads fighting stances on sight
Identified Brynjar's collarbone gap as a liability against a patient opponent
Offered it as professional courtesy, not threat
Movement at the south fork before dawn — two people, direction south toward Stillhold road
ragnar-solmyr.statusNote: Walking south on the Stillhold road trail with Brynjar and Freki.

notes: [d1] Brynjar arrived in the Marchward on World-day 20, early morning. First impressions: Redline warrior on bench near Wardhouse, Stillhold woman in grey, waygate anchor-stone visible but avoided by locals.

[d1] The east fork outside the Crow and Thistle side door — muddy, boot-prints visible in the melt. Freki's trail confirms the south branch leads toward the Stillhold road; Ragnar saw two people here before dawn.

central-square.name: Central Square
central-square.note: Wide mud-packed junction with a well, public notice board, access to the Wardhouse, tavern, and the waygate anchor-stone.

the-wardhouse.name: The Wardhouse
the-wardhouse.note: Low stone building on the north side of the square. Intake table, weapons rack, charcoal map. Truce-law check-in point for all Marchward arrivals.

the-crow-and-thistle.name: The Crow and Thistle
the-crow-and-thistle.note: The inn on the south side of the Marchward square. Doss keeps it. Common room is the real information hub — road-escort arrangements, gossip, and morning meals. Freki paused at the third stool from…

east-fork.name: East Fork
east-fork.note: The split in the east lane — north branch to the waygate approach, south branch to the Stillhold road. Visible boot-prints in the spring mud.

news.text: The Water Crisis stands at Discoloration across the valley: The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Communities murmur but cooperate.
Word reaches you: Sow killed cleanly in the north wood — boar sounder now reduced; piglets scattered north into deeper scrub (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Bull boar taken on the scrub oak ridge north of Millbrook — the hunt party completed the job, both animals down. (near millbrook)

This reaches your area — Fendt has left the Edge District and is walking to Millbrook — his testimony names Overseer Grael explicitly; when he arrives, the paper becomes a political event in both settlements. (near radiant plateau edge)
This reaches your area — Two road opportunists at the limestone bend were disarmed and sent to Mara Wells in Millbrook for honest work — Silas Weir's name given as introduction (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Compound rune-work — Wither and The Raised Thing — bound an eleven-year rising in the old Ashwarden warden post at Raven's Home. (near gen-ashwarden-march-road)
The Water Crisis stands at First Sickness across the valley: Upstream fisher families fall ill. Boiling water becomes universal. Harmonic and Radiant leaders trade public accusations.

Redline Warrior: Over three days of sparring on the neutral ground, Redline Warrior has drawn two other visiting duelists into a loose, unspoken rivalry — testing footwork and reading each other without anyone yet naming the challenge openly.

recentOffers.thing: Doss watching Brynjar with the stillness of someone deciding something — she has not offered it yet, but she is on the edge of it
recentOffers.from: what Doss fears: the lie she does not catch in time, the guest who reads as safe and is not — and Freki just passed her test by reading true

recentOffers.thing: Ragnar volunteered that he saw movement at the east fork before dawn — a detail he'd been holding, waiting for someone who earned it
recentOffers.from: His nature as a reader of patterns and his days watching the square — he notices, and he gives what he notices to someone who shows up right

form: Stocky, average-height man with pale skin and icy blue eyes. Chestnut hair shaved at sides and back, long on top and knotted. Thick beard with two braids flanking the mouth. A dark blue Norse dragon-knot tattoo curls from behind his ear to his temple; vertical dark blue rune tattoos descend from above his brows, over both eyes and cheeks, into the beard. Blue tunic with light red cuff-and-collar accents, dark grey puffy trousers, dark blue shin-wraps over leather boots. Silver arm ring with two dragon heads on the right arm. Bronze-inlaid one-handed axe at the hip, seax at the back of the belt, pouches at the front.

bio.hometown: A northern village called Javiksholm but it was raided and razed.
bio.residence: They’ve been on the road joined by their hound Freki
bio.livelihood: Hunting and trading on the road
bio.motivation: They were forced to flee from the raid that killed their father and razed their home village.
bio.story: Brynjar had fled from his village of Javiksholm during the raid as his father commanded. On his way out of the longhouse he made sure to grab his axe and shield. He also was followed by his dog Freki. Before he had fully escaped the village he looked back towards the longhouse and saw his father Andyr fighting off many of the raiders until one, the leader of this raid and a rival Jarl Kjotve Olafsson, who wanted the land Brynjar’s clan had owned due to its fertile fields and prime oceanfront access. Kjotve had locked into a duel with Andyr until he started loosing and he had one of his men strike him in the back with a spear, then it was all over. Kjotve had ordered his men to toss his body into the ocean and burn the longhouse down. After seeing this Brynjar had fled and escaped from the village. He has been on the road ever since surviving off of hunting wild game and trading with villages and traders he passed along the way, which leads us here.

the-marchward.label: The Marchward
facts: [d1] A border town between the Redline and the Stillhold — Redliners and Stillholders use the same square without warmth. A waygate anchor-stone sits at the corner under truce-law; no one stands near it. The Wardhouse is the visible seat of local authority.

[d1] Three bills posted: a filled labor posting for road-clearing east toward the Stillhold; a waygate truce-law notice requiring all arrivals to present at the Wardhouse within the hour with arms checked; and a missing-person posting for Sera of the Stillhold — grey cloak, dark braid, last seen at the…
[d?] you have walked here
[d1] All arrivals must present at the Wardhouse within the hour by truce-law; arms are checked on the rack inside. The duty officer logs names, origins, and any declaration of blood business.

[d1] The Wardhouse posts a weekly night-watch vacancy for travelers without permanent standing — meal, floor space, and a half-copper per watch. Road-clearing east toward the Stillhold is already taken.

kjotve-olafsson.label: Kjotve Olafsson
facts: [d1] Jarl who led the raid on Javiksholm. Won Andyr's land by having a spearman strike Andyr from behind mid-duel — a formal fight turned to murder when Kjotve was losing. Now holds Javiksholm's fields and shore. Andyr's body was thrown into the ocean.
[d1] met in play

javiksholm.label: Javiksholm
facts: [d1] Brynjar's home village — raided and razed by Kjotve Olafsson. Andyr died here, struck from behind. The longhouse was burned. Kjotve now holds the fertile fields and the oceanfront.

brynjar-andyrsson-thread.label: The Oath on Kjotve
facts: [d1] Brynjar swore aloud, to his father and the gods, to kill Kjotve Olafsson — but to do it with more honor than Kjotve ever showed. The oath is spoken and cannot be unsaid.

sera-of-the-stillhold.label: Sera of the Stillhold
facts: [d1] Missing woman, grey cloak, dark braid, last seen at the Marchward's south gate seven days ago. Her family posted a bill — no payment, no questions asked of the finder. An ink portrait is attached.

[d1] Arrived alone seven days ago — unusual; checked in properly, no declared business, asked for lodging at the Crow and Thistle. Room empty next morning, innkeep Doss didn't see her leave. Family posted the bill knowing roughly why she came but wanting her back without questions.

[d1] Sera left the Crow and Thistle before dawn of her own will — room latched from inside, side door. She spent her evening watching the entrance, thinking-quiet, waiting or watching for someone. Something was left at the third stool from the left at the bar around the same time.

[d1] A second person was present the evening Sera stayed — sat at the third stool, watched her without speaking, left before dawn, left behind a folded linen with a dried white-flowered sprig and a knotted cord. The cord-knot's meaning is unknown. Freki reads the stool as a live trail.

[d1] Freki read the cloth bundle — dried white-flowered sprig and knotted cord — and tracked toward the side door Sera used before dawn. The trail leads east, to a fork: waygate approach north, Stillhold road south.

[d1] Freki confirmed the trail at the fork goes south — toward the Stillhold road. Ragnar saw two people at the south fork before full light. Sera was not alone when she left, or someone followed her.

[d1] Two sets of prints at the south fork: a lighter frame went first, a heavier follower came ten minutes behind. Sera was not alone — someone tracked her, or met her, on the Stillhold road. Time of passage: roughly three to four hours before the Marchward woke.

[d1] Freki confirmed the lighter prints as Sera's — she was moving fast, not walking. Ragnar reads it as someone who knows where they're going or doesn't want to be seen; the gait is the same either way.

[d1] The heavier follower's prints read as deliberate pursuit — weight forward, toe-heavy, pace quickening as sight-lines opened south. Not a soldier, not a hunter. Someone closing distance on a target they knew was ahead.

companion-bristle.label: Freki
facts: [d?] The Stray That Stayed — Wary, watchful, keeping a body-length of distance even as it follows — a dog deciding whether the world is safe again.

the-last-walker.label: The Last Walker of the Sealed Wood
facts: [d1] [while away] The Last Walker has moved no further than the wood's edge, standing long hours in the threshold light — the tension between enclosure and the open world unresolved, no step taken in either direction.

halvra-stenn.label: Halvra Stenn
facts: [d1] Wardhouse Duty Officer — she processes what the Marchward receives, and she has received a great deal
[d1] Wardhouse intake officer, the Marchward

crow-and-thistle.label: The Crow and Thistle
facts: [d1] The inn where Doss works and where informal road-escort arrangements get made before anyone posts a formal notice. The common room is the real information hub of the Marchward.
[d1] The side door opens onto a back lane heading east — north fork leads to the waygate approach, south fork joins the Stillhold road. This is the door Sera and the bundle-leaver both used.

doss.label: Doss
facts: [d1] Innkeep, the Crow and Thistle
[d1] Doss is the innkeep — reads strangers fast and without warmth she hasn't decided to spend. Freki paused at the third stool from the left; something there.
[d1] Innkeep at the Crow and Thistle. Named Brynjar's two purposes before he stated them. Watched Freki more carefully than she watched Brynjar.
[d1] Innkeep of the Crow and Thistle — she has outlasted three wars and two owners, and the place is hers by survival

ragnar-solmyr.label: Ragnar Solmyr
facts: [d1] Redline Warrior, visiting the Marchward neutral ground

[d1] His name is Ragnar Solmyr — a Redline Warrior visiting the Marchward neutral ground. He saw movement at the east fork before dawn and knows the Stillhold road four miles out. Committed to search for Sera without hesitation when asked directly.
[d1] Marcher duelist, visiting the Marchward neutral ground

[d1] [while away] Over three days of sparring on the neutral ground, Redline Warrior has drawn two other visiting duelists into a loose, unspoken rivalry — testing footwork and reading each other without anyone yet naming the challenge openly.

[d1] His name is Ragnar Solmyr — a Redline Warrior visiting the Marchward neutral ground. He saw movement at the east fork before dawn and knows the Stillhold road four miles out. Committed to search for Sera without hesitation when asked directly.

entries.why: a hand in this people's craft
the far side of the circle from your own

standingLedger.text: Your birth and training are known: marcher +4, somatic +2, lattice +1, stillhold -1.
approached a Redline Warrior with plain speech and a named purpose — handled the form correctly

personalArc.name: The Thread of A Northern Village Called Javiksholm But It Was Raided And Razed.
personalArc.premise: Brynjar Andyrsson carries an unfinished thing: They were forced to flee from the raid that killed their father and razed their home village.. The valley will give them the chance to face it.
personalArc.stakes: who they become when the thing they left behind finally catches up

stages.objective: a first sign of They were forced to flee from the raid that killed their father and razed their home village. surfaces in the valley
the thread deepens — what it costs to pull it becomes clear

routes.marcher: answer it as your people would
routes.seek: seek the truth of it, wherever it leads

personalArc.notes_for_gm: A light personal thread seeded from the character's backstory (SNG-133 fallback) — a slow gravity across sessions; the ending is the player's, never foreclosed.

wardhouse-officer.name: Halvra Stenn
wardhouse-officer.role: Wardhouse Duty Officer — she processes what the Marchward receives, and she has received a great deal
wardhouse-officer.voiceHints: Unhurried, precise, neither cold nor warm — the voice of a woman who has asked the same necessary questions ten thousand times and still means every one. She will wait for a real answer. She has a drawer full of time. Oaths do not impress her; she has seen oaths arrive on men who bled out before sundown. What she wants to know is what you actually are.
knowledge: Every entry protocol the Marchward has written or revised in the last eighteen years, including the three that were written because of things she personally witnessed
How to read an arrival — what a man's posture, kit, and silence say about what he did before he reached this door
That sworn fighters carrying edge-oaths come through in clusters, and that the clusters predict things, and that she has logged every cluster since she took this post
The early-spring thaw makes the northern passes passable two weeks before most arrivals expect, which means the Wardhouse is always short-staffed for the first surge
wardhouse-officer.wants: Honest answers to honest questions, and an orderly log at end of shift.
wardhouse-officer.fears: Waving through the thing that unmakes the Marchward because she asked the wrong question — or worse, the right question and accepted a careful lie.

reactsToReputation.oath-bound: takes note, writes it down, asks what the oath costs you rather than what it grants
reactsToReputation.violent: does not flinch — adjusts the question, not the composure
reactsToReputation.evasive: quieter, slower, more precise — she will find the edge of what you will not say and she will name it aloud

wardhouse-officer.roleFunction: reveal — surfaces what an arrival actually is beneath what they present; ward — holds the threshold against things that should not pass it unmarked
wardhouse-officer.roleMethod: Marchward method — truth extracted through bureaucratic patience, not pressure; the form is the instrument
wardhouse-officer.appearance: A compact woman somewhere in her mid-forties, hair the colour of old iron worn in a single braid wound close to the head. A small, neat scar through the left eyebrow — not a fighter's scar, an old workplace scar, which tells its own story. Wears the Wardhouse grey, kept better than the building deserves. Her hands are ink-stained at the fingertips and absolutely still when she is waiting for your answer.
wardhouse-officer.domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Halvra practises verist as craft — truth as the working material of duty, not ideology. Marcher secondary because she holds a threshold and knows what holding costs. Syllogist tertiary: the log, the form, the deduction from incomplete data.

arrivalProtocol.standardQuestion: Name, origin, purpose in the Marchward, and anything you are carrying that could end a conversation permanently.
arrivalProtocol.oathQuestion: She asks it simply, without ceremony: 'What does the oath require of you while you are here?' — because she needs to know whether what you swore will make you easier or harder to process when something goes wrong.
arrivalProtocol.noteOnBrynjar: She asked him a question that required a real answer. She is still waiting. She has written nothing yet.

provenance.hint: Wardhouse duty officer — a woman who processes arrivals with bureaucratic calm and quiet competence; she has seen fighte
provenance.why: She is the first functional authority figure Brynjar speaks to in the Marchward, and she asked a question that requires

doss.name: Doss Halveck
doss.role: Innkeep of the Crow and Thistle — she has outlasted three wars and two owners, and the place is hers by survival
doss.voiceHints: Unhurried and direct — speaks the way she sets a plate down, no wasted motion. Does not soften things because softening things has cost her before. Reads the room like a ledger: totals you up before you have found a seat. When she decides you are all right she does not say so; she simply refills your cup without being asked.
knowledge: That Sera of the Stillhold slept here three nights ago, took the north-facing room, ate very little, and left before first light on the fourth day without a word — which tells Doss exactly what it means
Everyone who passes through the Marchward trade road sooner or later sits at one of her tables; she knows what fear smells like by faction
Which of the regulars are being paid to watch the road, and by whom, because she noticed when the coin changed
Early-spring road conditions north to the Reach — she collects that from drivers and drovers and keeps it current because her guests' lives depend on it
Folk-level stillcraft: enough to recognise it in a guest, not enough to practise it, and she does not pretend otherwise
doss.wants: A house that holds — the Crow and Thistle as the one place in the Marchward where a traveller can sleep without one eye open, and she will enforce that with a broken-bottle smile if she must.
doss.fears: The lie she does not catch in time — the guest who reads as safe and is not, and someone pays for her misjudgement.

reactsToReputation.honest: gives you the real information, not the polite version
reactsToReputation.violent: serves you, watches you, and has already decided where the trouble ends if you start it
reactsToReputation.seeking: asks one pointed question and lets the answer tell her whether you are worth helping
reactsToReputation.cowardly: no contempt, but no patience either — the Marchward does not carry passengers

doss.universalRole: Keeper — holds the threshold, reads who crosses it, decides what passes
doss.roleMethod: Marchward method — keeps peace through blunt honesty and the credible promise of consequence
doss.functionsNote: reveal: surfaces information about Sera's movements and road conditions; shield: the inn is a protected threshold — she enforces that materially; bind: her word given on a guest's safety is a local obligation both sides of the Marchward understand
doss.appearance: A stout woman in her mid-fifties, broad through the shoulders, heavy-handed in the way of someone who has carried kegs since she was fifteen. Skin the colour of old oak, short grey hair kept close to the skull for practicality. A crescent scar at the left jaw from a long-ago disagreement. Apron is clean — she changes it twice a day on principle. Eyes the flat, still grey of a whetstone, and about as yielding.
doss.seasonalNote: Early spring: the Crow and Thistle is packed tighter than usual — road crews sheltering between the last frost and first mud, drovers waiting on passable ground. Doss is short on sleep and shorter on tolerance for delay.
doss.domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). verist primary: her house runs on truth told plainly — she will not lie to a guest and will not be lied to. marcher secondary: the Marchward grain is in her bone; she holds a line when she must. somatic tertiary: the body's ledger — fatigue, fear, hunger; she reads these in guests and manages her own without complaint.
doss.curriculumNote: Not a formal teacher. Will answer a direct question directly. What she withholds is the shape of what she has seen — that requires trust, and trust requires time, and she is the one who decides when there has been enough of both.

provenance.hint: Doss, the innkeep at the Crow and Thistle — fifties, stout, sharp, reads strangers faster than they read themselves; kee
provenance.why: Doss is a named, established NPC the fiction has introduced as a central information source for the Sera thread and road

establishedFacts.text: Brynjar has sworn a blood oath to kill Kjotve Olafsson with honor — spoken aloud in the Marchward square on World-day 20.

chronicleCache.hash: d0|b|c|q|L1|They were forced to flee from the raid that kill
chronicleCache.text: You have become a man unmade and remaking — your father Andyr's blood still vivid in memory, the village ash-smell still catching in your throat when the wind turns wrong, the night Jarl Kjotve Olafsson's raiders came and you ran still a splinter you cannot pull clean. What you have done since is small but absolute: you opened your palm, pressed steel to skin, and swore Andyr's name against Kjotve's, a blood oath that has given your flight the shape of a purpose. The Marchward knows your face now, and the Marchers know it too — a grieving son moving through their territory with a wound he has decided to sharpen into something useful.

bristle.text: Approached a Redline Warrior as a peer, named the task plainly, and earned his name and his company

corrections.why: player: same person (Redline Warrior → Ragnar Solmyr)
